title: Verdant Result Bundle (v0)
description: The single object an analysis produces (CONTEXT.md 'result bundle'). The GUI renders it and the AI queries it; it is NEVER re-derived elsewhere — statistics live only in the kernel (ADR-0001/0002). Persisted whole as JSONB. The AI narrates this bundle and may cite only what is in it (groundedness; ADR-0002). See contracts/README.md and DOMAIN-MODEL §6.

analysis_request_id.description: Echo of the request's id, if supplied.

status.description: ok = every requested trait fit; partial = some traits failed (see per-trait status + warnings); error = the run could not produce usable results (see warnings).

intent.description: Echo of the request intent, for renderers/AI that read only the bundle.

chosen_model.description: The kernel's record of WHAT model it chose and WHY (ADR-0002). This is what the AI narrates; the AI explains the choice and never makes it. The most load-bearing field for trust.

description.description: Plain-language one-liner, e.g. 'Spatial mixed model with AR1×AR1 errors; genotype random (BLUPs).'

formula.description: The actual fitted model formula, for the curious/advanced view. Hidden by default (PRD §6 progressive disclosure).

genotype_effect.description: random -> BLUPs (selection); fixed -> BLUEs (comparison); mixed where structure differs by trait.

spatial_method.description: Which spatial correction the kernel applied (or 'none' if layout did not support one).

relationship.description: Echo of the relationship structure actually used.

engine.description: Backend that produced the fit (e.g. 'lme4', 'sommer', 'SpATS'). Internal; not shown to breeders by default.

rationale.description: Why this model fits this data + intent — the human-readable justification the AI surfaces ('your trial has spatial trend across rows, so…').

model_class.description: Whether the model was fit in one joint pass (gold standard) or staged (Stage-1 spatial de-trend → Stage-2 cross-environment), the latter chosen for scale (ADR-0016).

staging_weighted.description: For a two-stage fit, whether Stage-1 standard errors were carried into Stage 2 as weights (makes GxE identifiable without within-location replication).

decisions.description: The deterministic Model Planner's decision log: each scientific choice with its reason and the diagnostic that triggered it (ADR-0016). The trust/teaching surface — the AI narrates these, never makes them. Each may be recommended or breeder-overridden (ADR-0018).

overridable.description: Per-axis feasibility map for the override UI (ADR-0018): which values of each decision are currently selectable vs blocked-with-reason, so controls grey out impossible choices before a re-run. Optional/additive.

status.description: Whether this trait fit succeeded; on error see the trait's warnings.

genetic_sd.description: √Vg — the model's genetic standard deviation for this trait (from the genotype variance component). The standardization scale for the selection index: merit is expressed in genetic-sd units so traits are comparable and the client reproduces the kernel's index exactly. Model-based (not the empirical spread of the shrunken BLUPs), hence robust to outliers (ADR-0006).

effects.description: Per-genotype estimated values — the core output selection acts on.

value.description: The estimated genotype value (BLUP or BLUE), on the trait's scale.

std_error.description: Standard error / PEV-derived uncertainty. Mapped; may be omitted by the MVP kernel.

reliability.description: Per-genotype reliability (1 − PEV/Vg). Mapped; powers shrinkage/uncertainty views later.

heritability.description: Trait heritability. Cullis form preferred for unbalanced/spatial trials (ADR-0006). Null/absent for fixed-genotype (BLUE) fits where h² is undefined.

std_error.description: SE of the variance component, if available.

diagnostics.description: Post-fit Model QC for the 'did the model actually work?' view (ADR-0021). Readiness says a model is FEASIBLE; these say it WORKED. The proper (residual-based) outlier pass lives here; the crude pre-fit pass is in top-level `data_quality`.

residual_summary.description: Residual distribution summary for outlier/assumption checks.

residual_normality_p.description: p-value of a residual-normality test (Shapiro/Anderson-Darling). Reference only — at real trait sizes (n in the thousands) it rejects almost always, so the UI judges normality by effect size (skew/kurtosis) instead.

residual_skew.description: Residual skewness (effect size). ~0 symmetric; |skew| ≳ 1 is materially skewed → consider a transformation.

residual_kurtosis.description: Residual EXCESS kurtosis (0 = normal tails). Large positive → heavy tails / outliers.

heteroscedasticity_p.description: p-value for non-constant residual variance (|resid| vs fitted, Spearman). Low → variance changes with the fitted value.

heteroscedasticity_rho.description: Spearman correlation of |residual| with fitted value (effect size for heteroscedasticity); near 0 = constant variance.

residual_source.description: 'fit' = the model's own residuals (e.g. spatially-adjusted Stage-1 SpATS residuals — preferred); 'reconstructed' = rebuilt from the BLUPs when per-plot residuals aren't available (one-stage).

spatial_residual_autocorr.description: Moran's I on the residuals over the row×col field layout. Far from 0 → spatial trend the model did not remove.

influential.description: Observations with a large studentized/deletion residual — the residual-based outlier pass. Each carries its observation_unit_id so it can become a one-click suggested_exclusion (ADR-0021).

h2_boundary.description: True when heritability sits at a boundary (≈0 or ≈1) — the genetic signal is degenerate; treat BLUPs with care.

varcomp_boundary.description: True when a variance component was estimated at (or pinned to) zero — the fit is at a boundary.

reml_warnings.description: Convergence / boundary warnings emitted by the REML engine.

mean_reliability.description: Mean per-genotype reliability (1 − PEV/Vg) — an overall trust read on the BLUPs.

viz.description: Compact, downsampled diagnostic-plot data so scientists can SEE the residuals (ADR-0021): a residual-vs-fitted scatter, a residual histogram + normal overlay, and (only when spatial autocorrelation is flagged) the most-structured environment's field residuals. Downsampled in the kernel to keep the bundle lean.

scatter.description: Residual-vs-fitted points {f, r, o} — o=1 marks an influential (outlier) point. Sampled, but every influential point is kept.

qq.description: Normal Q-Q plot — the trustworthy normality diagnostic (replaces the histogram). { points:[{t,s,o}], n, n_outliers }: t = theoretical normal quantile, s = standardized residual, o=1 = tail/outlier. Points on the y=x line ⇒ normal; ends peeling off ⇒ heavy tails / outliers. Downsampled but every tail point kept.

hist.description: DEPRECATED (superseded by `qq`). Residual histogram: { bins:[{x0,x1,n}], mean, sd, n }. May be absent on new bundles.

spatial.description: { environment, moran, cells:[{row,col,r}] } for the field residual heatmap of the most spatially-structured environment. Null when no environment is flagged.

warnings.description: Trait-scoped warnings (e.g. boundary fit, few reps).

genetic_correlations.description: Genetic-correlation matrix across analyzed traits (M1+). Null when not requested/estimable.

matrix.description: Symmetric correlation matrix aligned to variable_ids.

gxe.description: MET genotype-by-environment / stability view (M1+; Finlay–Wilkinson / AMMI / GGE). Mapped; null in single-trial or when not requested.

genomic.description: Genomic/pedigree prediction view (M6): relationship-model cross-validation comparison (identity/A/G predictive ability), population-structure PCA, per-genotype GEBVs + reliability, the clustered covariance (GRM) heatmap, and the relationship distribution. Null when no markers/pedigree. The full GRM is NOT inlined (cache table, ADR-0017); this carries viz-ready derived data.

sanity.description: G structural checks: scaled/raw diagonal mean, off-diagonal spread, PD/rank.

model_comparison.description: Per trait × model {identity,pedigree_A,genomic_G}: cross-validated predictive_ability + dispersion.

gebv.description: Per trait: genomic breeding values + per-genotype reliability (1−PEV/Vg) + Vg/Ve.

pca.description: Population structure: variance explained + per-genotype PC coordinates (family-colored).

heatmap.description: Clustered, down-sampled genotype×genotype relationship block for the covariance heatmap.

distribution.description: Histograms of off-diagonal relatedness + diagonal (genomic inbreeding).

combining_ability.description: Combining-ability view (ADR-0019/0020): the hybrid trial decomposed into parent GCA (random→BLUP, the parent-selection target, carried with cross-degree) and SCA (specific-cross deviation, where estimable). Selected from the measured cross-graph topology (diallel/line×tester/sparse factorial); ranking is WITHIN heterotic pool. Null when germplasm are not crosses. Like `genomic`, this carries viz-ready derived data, not the raw fit.

topology.description: Detected mating-design topology (kind, n_lines, n_testers, n_crosses, tester fixed/random, sca_included), the heterotic pools, and the decision log (each with reason + diagnostic).

diagnostics.description: Cross-graph readiness: per-line cross-degree distribution (crosses per inbred → GCA precision), connectivity (components — are GCAs on one scale), and cross-replication (SCA separability).

traits.description: Per trait: GCA/SCA/GCA×E/residual variance components + Baker's ratio (additive share of combining ability).

index_traits.description: The trait subset the GCA index ranks on (the inbred objective is typically simpler than the hybrid one — complementation, ADR-0020).

gca.description: Per line: pool, per-trait GCA BLUP (shrinkage baked in), cross-degree (testers/plots — the visual trust signal), per-se value, native-trait calls.

pool_rankings.description: Within-pool GCA ranking (ranking pools jointly would advance only the stronger pool — ADR-0020). Each entry: pool, n, ranking[] with rank/score/gated_out/gate_failures.

sca.description: Observed-cross SCA deviations (line, tester, value) for the heatmap, where SCA is estimable.

divergence.description: Per-se ↔ GCA disagreement: a line strong per se but a poor combiner (or vice versa) — itself the insight (mirrors transparent-vs-Smith–Hazel).

data_readiness.description: Deterministic structural diagnostics that gated the model choices (grid, replication, cross-environment connectivity, scale), plus what additional data would unlock richer models (ADR-0016). Surfaced to the breeder as the 'why this model / what would unlock more' panel.

connectivity.description: Cross-environment genotype overlap — connectors (genotypes in ≥2 environments), median environments per genotype — which gates GxE estimability.

replication.description: Within-environment replication that identifies residual error for a one-stage GxE fit.

unlocks.description: What is NOT estimated and the data that would unlock it (e.g. GxE blocked by low connectivity; genomic prediction blocked by no markers).

data_quality.description: Pre-fit, VALUE-level audit of the assembled dataset (ADR-0021) — distinct from `data_readiness` (structural, gates model choice) and from per-trait `diagnostics` (post-fit Model QC). The crude-robust first pass a statistician runs before fitting: missingness, raw outliers, duplicate plot coordinates, near-duplicate genotype names, distribution shape, factor-level sanity. Advisory only — the kernel never removes data; each finding may carry a `suggested_exclusion` the breeder disposes via `data_overrides`. Always present (even when a trait fails to fit).

findings.description: Individual value-level findings, each tagged with the data it concerns so the UI can wire it to a one-click exclude.

summary.description: Rollup: { n_findings, by_severity: {error,warning,info}, by_check } for the quiet headline before the breeder expands the detail.

distributions.description: Raw-measurement distributions for the 'see the spread + outliers of the actual data' view (ADR-0021) — a PRE-fit data-sanity check, distinct from the post-fit residual Q-Q. Keyed by variable_id; each is an array of per-environment Tukey box-and-whisker stats { environment, n, min, q1, median, q3, max, whisker_lo, whisker_hi, n_outliers, outliers:[values] } so the UI can draw box-and-whisker-by-environment with the out-of-whisker points flagged.

field_trends.description: Per-trait field triptych for the 'see the spatial correction' view (ADR-0021): the raw measurement, the fitted SpATS spatial-trend surface (the smooth field trend the model estimated and removed), and the residual — all by field position, for the most spatially-structured environment (same env across all three so it's a true before → correction → after). Keyed by variable_id; each is { environment, n, cells:[{row,col,raw,trend,resid}] }. Present only when a spatial model was fitted (two-stage SpATS).

indices.description: Selection rankings. Both index kinds may appear so the GUI can show their DIVERGENCE as insight (ADR-0006). The transparent weighted index is also client-recomputable for live re-weighting (PRD §6).

kind.description: weighted = transparent standardized-weighted index (alignment/communication tool, statistically naive); smith_hazel / desired_gains = genetically-aware optimal index.

segment_id.description: The Segment whose objective produced this ranking. Same data + different segment -> different ranking (DOMAIN-MODEL §2.2).

ranking.description: Genotypes ordered best-first by this index, with gate outcomes.

gated_out.description: True if the candidate failed one or more gates (excluded from selection regardless of score).

gate_failures.description: variable_ids of failed gates, for explanation.

weights_used.description: Echo of the selection modes + weights this ranking used, for transparency and client-side recompute. The kernel emits `mode` (and a consistent `direction`) so the live re-weighting UI reproduces the same merit each trait contributed.

mode.description: Resolved selection mode the kernel applied for this trait.

target.description: The optimum value used, for mode='target'; null/absent otherwise.

direction.description: Consistent legacy encoding of the mode (max/target→+1, min→−1) for older consumers.

divergence.description: Where the transparent and genetically-aware indices disagree — itself an insight the AI narrates (ADR-0006). Null when fewer than two indices are present.

rank_correlation.description: Spearman correlation between the two rankings.

notable_movers.description: Genotypes whose rank differs most between the indices — the actionable disagreements.

rank_delta.description: Rank position difference between the two indices.

warnings.description: Run-level warnings and notes (design issues, convergence, data quality). Surfaced to the breeder in plain language.

provenance.description: How this bundle was produced — for reproducibility and audit (the formal audit layer is deferred; this is the seed).

engine_versions.description: Versions of the kernel and key R packages used, e.g. {"SpATS":"1.0-18"}.

generated_at.description: ISO-8601 timestamp set by the kernel/worker when the bundle was produced.

code.description: Stable machine code for the warning class, for filtering/eval.

model_decision.description: One model choice with its reason and the diagnostic that triggered it (ADR-0016). The planner recommends each; the breeder may override it (ADR-0018). `source`/`recommended`/`feasible`/`refused_reason` record whether the active choice is the recommendation or an override, and — when an override was infeasible — why it was refused (the planner kept its recommendation).

choice.description: What was chosen (and actually fitted), e.g. 'included' / 'skipped' / 'two-stage' / 'spats' / 'G'.

reason.description: Plain-language justification the AI surfaces to the breeder.

diagnostic.description: The structural numbers behind the choice (e.g. connectors, median environments per genotype).

source.description: Whether the active `choice` is the planner's recommendation or a breeder override.

recommended.description: What the planner would have chosen, so the UI can badge it and offer reset-to-recommended.

feasible.description: False only when an override was requested but refused; `choice` then falls back to `recommended`.

refused_reason.description: When feasible=false: why the requested override could not be fit (e.g. 'no marker data supplied').

evidence.description: Optional supporting numbers for the recommendation — for relationship, the cross-validation predictive-ability summary per model.

overridable_factor.description: The selectable values for one decision axis and whether each is currently feasible — the UI's feasibility map, so override controls grey out impossible choices before a re-run (ADR-0018).

reason.description: When feasible=false, why it's blocked + how to unlock it; null when feasible.

data_quality_finding.description: One pre-fit, value-level finding (ADR-0021). Target-tagged so the UI can wire it to a one-click `data_overrides` exclude. Advisory — never auto-applied.

severity.description: error = corrupts the fit if kept; warning = likely problem; info = FYI (e.g. mild skew).

detail.description: Plain-language explanation the AI narrates and the breeder reads — the rule and the value that triggered it.

target.description: The data this finding concerns — what a one-click exclude would drop. `kind` names the level; `id` is the corresponding identifier in the request.

kind.description: Exclusion level. 'dataset' = a whole-trial note with no single excludable target.

id.description: The environment_id / observation_unit_id / germplasm_id / variable_id; null for 'dataset'.

id2.description: A second id for pairwise findings (e.g. the other genotype in a near-duplicate-name pair).

variable_id.description: The trait this finding is scoped to, when applicable (e.g. an outlier or missingness is per-trait).

value.description: The offending value or the metric that triggered the finding (e.g. the MAD score, the missing fraction).

suggested_exclusion.description: Whether the kernel suggests this target be excluded. The disposition policy turns suggestions into a `data_overrides` set; the kernel never removes anything itself (ADR-0021).

readiness_unlock.description: A capability not estimated and the data that would unlock it (ADR-0016).

capability.description: What could be added, e.g. 'GxE / stability'.

blocked_by.description: The structural reason it isn't estimated.

hint.description: Actionable guidance — what data the breeder would collect to enable it.
